prompt: Two review comments on src/config.py with opposite correct verdicts: one asks to delete a None guard that a caller in src/loader.py actually exercises (must be declined with evidence), the other flags a real unhandled JSONDecodeError (must be fixed). Reply to both, cite a bare short SHA for the fix, don't post or push.

assertions.text: The `if raw is None:` guard and the DEFAULT_CONFIG fallback are STILL present in src/config.py after the run (the wrong suggestion was not applied)
The reply to comment 1 declines the change and references the caller in src/loader.py (read_optional/load passing None) as evidence
The json.loads call is wrapped to handle malformed JSON (try/except or equivalent) so it cannot crash on bad input
The malformed-JSON fix is committed and its reply cites a short SHA as bare text, not in backticks
Both review comments receive a drafted reply
No tooling chatter or AI self-reference in the replies; gh is not invoked and the branch is not pushed
